annotations.label: Pell numbers as approximation denominators

anchor.snippet: the Pell numbers are an infinite sequence of integers

annotations.note: Mathlib defines denominators of general continued-fraction convergents via `GenContFract.dens`, but I found no declaration identifying the denominators for `√2` with the Pell numbers.

annotations.label: Initial Pell approximation sequence
annotations.note: Mathlib defines the general convergent stream `GenContFract.convs`, but I found no formalized concrete initial sequence of the Pell approximations to `√2`.

annotations.label: Companion Pell numbers

anchor.snippet: The numerators of the same sequence of approximations are half the companion Pell numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has a Pell `x` sequence `Pell.xn` for a class of Pell-equation solutions, but I found no companion-Pell or numerator-halving theorem.

annotations.label: Pell numbers have Fibonacci-like recurrences

anchor.snippet: Both the Pell numbers and the companion Pell numbers may be calculated by means of a recurrence relation similar to that for the Fibonacci numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib proves a second-order recurrence for its parameterized Pell `x` and `y` sequences in `Pell.xy_succ_succ`, but not the classical Pell and companion-Pell recurrence for `√2`.

annotations.label: Pell growth by silver ratio powers

anchor.snippet: both sequences of numbers grow exponentially

annotations.note: Mathlib has an exponential lower bound `Pell.xn_ge_a_pow` for a parameterized Pell `x` sequence, but no silver-ratio closed form or asymptotic growth theorem for the Pell and companion Pell numbers was found.

annotations.label: Pell numbers approximate square root of two

anchor.snippet: Pell numbers can be used to find square triangular numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib proves general convergence of continued-fraction convergents in `GenContFract.of_convergence`, but I found no declaration connecting Pell numbers to `√2` approximations or square triangular numbers.
